title: Picrophloeus rugulosus
taxonomy: Picrophloeus rugulosus is a member of the plant family Euphorbiaceae and is classified under the kingdom Plantae. It belongs to the order Malpighiales, a group of flowering plants that are commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. The species is further classified into the genus Picrophloeus, a monotypic genus that consists of a single species. Picrophloeus rugulosus is native to the mountainous regions of the Mediterranean and is often found growing in rocky and sandy areas. The classification of Picrophloeus rugulosus is an important aspect of its identification and understanding of its evolutionary history.
uses: Picrophloeus rugulosus, commonly known as the Rough-stemmed Pyrenean Rampion, has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits. The plant's leaves and roots are said to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help in alleviating symptoms of various ailments. Some studies suggest that Picrophloeus rugulosus may have antimicrobial and antifungal properties, making it a potential natural remedy for infections. However, further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption. More studies are required to fully explore the medicinal uses of Picrophloeus rugulosus.

side_effects: Picrophloeus rugulosus, also known as the rough-leaved rock-rose, has been reported to have potential side effects due to its toxic compounds.
